item.title: A Restricted Boltzmann Machine in R
item.author: kmckee90@gmail.com (Kevin McKee)
item.description: github source for this project For most statistical models, you can verify that they work by simulating data from a set of parameter values, then fitting the model to the simulated data and seeing how well it recovers those values. With artificial neural networks it is common to have many local solutions and a stochastic learning algorithm, so while an ANN may find an good solution on the simulated data, it is far less likely to match the data-generating parameters.

item.title: Painting a harpsichord lid
item.author: kmckee90@gmail.com (Kevin McKee)
item.description: Some process photos from my painting on a harpsichord lid. (I’m using this post to figure out getting images into blog posts and such.) I painted this harpsichord lid for a commission in 2016. I started with a photoshop mockup: The first stages of the actual painting are interesting to look back on because they show how fundamental color and movement are to the composition: Final result:

item.title: First post
item.author: kmckee90@gmail.com (Kevin McKee)
item.description: As of this first post, I’ll be using this site to document my various projects, code, minor discoveries, or other thoughts that probably won’t be published anywhere else.

item.title: About me
item.author: kmckee90@gmail.com (Kevin McKee)
item.description: img { float: right; padding-bottom: 30px; } I have lived in Virginia for my whole life. As of recently, I live in a house out in the mountains with my wife, Linnea, my cat, Hobbes, and our new pup, Thomas (pictured above). Interests Psychology My undergraduate degree is in Psychology but I have not carried out much of my own substantive research. Having come to understand things like artificial neural networks, I am hoping to use them to eventually contribute to the science of cognition and perception.

item.title: Code
item.author: kmckee90@gmail.com (Kevin McKee)
item.description: Projects: A Restricted Boltzmann Machine in R All coding projects can be found on github. The links to posts for each project should show up under “See Also” on this page. Some of my projects are completely lacking in annotations, full of commented out zombie code, and generally messy. I’ll be in the process of cleaning them up as I write about them.

item.title: Publications

item.description: Google Scholar page Curriculum Vitae OSF for supplemental materials of published projects McKee, K. L., (2021) Hierarchical Biometrical Genetic Analysis of Dynamics. Behavior Genetics (pending final reviews) Preprint McKee, K. L., Crandell, I. C., Hanlon, A. L. (2020) US County-Level Social Distancing and Policy Impact: A Dynamical Systems Model. Journal of Medical Internet Research https://doi.org/10.2196/23902 McKee, K. L., Russell, M., Mennis, J., Mason, M., & Neale, M. C. (2019). Emotion Regulation Dynamics Predict Substance Use in High-Risk Adolescents.

item.title: Research
item.author: kmckee90@gmail.com (Kevin McKee)
item.description: Job title: Postdoctoral Associate Department of Statistics Center for Biostatistics and Health Data Science Virginia Tech Applied Statistics My primary role in research is as a statistical consultant, helping investigators with everything from the conception of new hypotheses to writing grants, running analyses, and publishing manuscripts. Theoretical Methods My methodological research merges ideas from biometrical genetic theory with state-space modeling. In short, I am interested in how estimated genetic signals such as heritability can be used to validate theoretical models of cognition and behavior.
